mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and demand, creating positive momentum in equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.15 over 1-year rolling windows, or the interaction term in regression models is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) during expansionary cycles signal strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined growth of RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.10 over 3-year rolling windows, or the interaction term in regression models is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 270 signals persistent inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 270, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns drops below -0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust job market conditions that support consumer confidence and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances with PAYEMS growth above 100k, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production combined with rising housing starts suggests supply-side bottlenecks offset by demand pressures, creating mixed signals that reduce S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.10 over 1-year rolling windows, or the interaction term in regression models is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) during periods of GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates sustained demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined growth of RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.15 over 3-year rolling windows, or the interaction term in regression models is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).
